payload.text: Build an app called AI Thumbnails Maker. The app generates professional YouTube video thumbnails. It should take two main inputs from the user: (1) the video title, and (2) a headshot photo of the YouTuber. Based on these inputs, the app should automatically create a thumbnail in 1080x1920 px (9:16 aspect ratio). The app should place the YouTuber’s headshot prominently, matching realistic facial expressions with the tone of the title, and combine it with bold, eye-catching typography for the video title. The design style should include high contrast, vibrant colors, glowing highlights, cinematic depth of field, and modern text placement to maximize click-through rate. The final thumbnail should look professional, scroll-stopping, and fully optimized for YouTube.”

**Defining the Scope**

I'm now focusing on defining the scope for "AI Thumbnails Maker." Currently, it seems the key inputs are a video title and a headshot. The ideal output is a 1280x720 thumbnail, but the output resolution might change per the user. I need to make sure to keep the scope as specified.


**Clarifying Aspect Ratio**

I've clarified the aspect ratio: it's 1080x1920 pixels, which translates to a 9:16 vertical format suitable for Shorts or Reels. The prompt clearly specifies this, so I will ensure the user understands it's for vertical videos. I'm focusing on the style guidelines: high contrast, vibrant colors, glowing highlights, cinematic depth of field, bold typography, and a professional, "scroll-stopping" look. I will require the model to place the headshot prominently and match expressions with the title's tone. I plan to use Gemini with image capabilities.
I'm setting up my project with `index.html`, `index.tsx`, and `App.tsx` which will handle state management for title, photo, and generation. I'm dividing the components into header, InputForm, ResultGallery, and Loader. I plan to integrate the Gemini service.


**Refining Gemini Prompt**

I'm now refining the prompt for Gemini. Key inputs remain a headshot and title. The tool will send these to `gemini-2.5-flash-image`. The updated prompt is now: "Generate a professional YouTube Shorts thumbnail (9:16 aspect ratio). Use the face in this image. The title is '[Title]'. It should be high-contrast, vibrant, cinematic, with glowing highlights and bold typography. Ensure the person's expression matches the title's tone." I am adding details about UI for YouTube Shorts. I'll test Gemini's text generation quality.

entries.description: Service for interacting with Gemini API for image generation.
Form for user input (title and photo).
Displays the generated thumbnail and download options.
Main application logic and state management.

payload.text:
Build an app called AI Thumbnails Maker. The app generates high-quality, professional YouTube Shorts thumbnails.

Inputs:
1) Video title
2) A clear headshot photo of the YouTuber

Output:
Create a 1080x1920 px (9:16) thumbnail optimized for YouTube Shorts.

Design & Composition:
• Generate a realistic upper-body portrait (head, shoulders, chest, and arms)
• Hands must be fully visible, natural, and anatomically correct (5 fingers per hand, correct proportions)
• Natural human posture (no twisted arms, no extra fingers, no distorted joints)
• Realistic body alignment and relaxed pose (pointing, thinking pose, crossed arms, or expressive gesture based on title)

Facial Expression & Pose Matching:
• Facial expression AND hand gesture must match the emotion of the video title
  – Motivation → confident pose, clenched fist or pointing
  – Shock → open hands, surprised expression
  – Study / focus → thoughtful pose, hand on chin
• Avoid robotic or stiff poses

Visual Quality:
• Ultra-realistic human anatomy
• Natural skin texture and lighting
• No artificial limbs, no deformed hands, no floating arms
• Cinematic depth of field with sharp subject focus
• Studio-quality lighting with soft shadows

Typography:
• Bold, modern, high-contrast text
• Clean, readable font optimized for mobile viewing
• Smart text placement that does NOT overlap face or hands
• Use minimal words (2–5 words maximum)

Style:
• Vibrant colors, high contrast
• Subtle glow highlights
• Professional YouTube creator aesthetic
• Scroll-stopping, clickable, realistic look

Negative Prompt (Strict):
• No extra fingers
• No distorted hands
• No unnatural body posture
• No blurred hands
• No cartoon or AI-generated look
• No cropped hands or arms

**Refining Image Dimensions**

I'm focused on the precise aspect ratio requested for the AI Thumbnail Maker. The previous version was close, but now I'm incorporating the exact 1080x1920 dimension and 9:16 ratio to meet the user's explicit requirements. This also affects the image generation component, to ensure no unintended cropping or distortion.


**Detailing Image Generation**

I'm now integrating highly specific instructions into the prompt for image generation. These cover facial expressions, hand gestures aligned to the title, and detailed poses. I'm also addressing typography rules to ensure clear, impactful text. Focusing on ultra-realistic style and sharp focus, I've incorporated negative prompts. I'm also considering upgrading to `gemini-3-pro-image-preview` for better quality, but will require user API keys.


**Prioritizing Prompt Engineering**

I'm currently prioritizing prompt engineering to leverage `gemini-2.5-flash-image` effectively. I've updated the prompt in `geminiService.ts` to include specific negative constraints and posture requirements, as well as typography guidelines. The UI might incorporate a "Pose" selector. The "hands visible" request is now directly integrated into the prompt instructions. I'm focusing on ensuring clear, impactful text.


**Adjusting Quality & Typography**

I'm now integrating the typography requirement of minimal words (2-5 maximum) directly into the image generation prompt within `geminiService.ts`, as well as considering how to surface this in the UI. Also, I'm adapting the `ResultView` to resemble a "YouTube Shorts" preview. I'm reaffirming the use of `gemini-2.5-flash-image` unless "high-quality" images are explicitly requested. The prompt will also include more anatomy constraints, emphasizing the pose's alignment with the title, and the typography details. I am also determining if `gemini-3-pro-image-preview` should be used instead.


**Revising API Key Handling**

I'm revisiting the API key logic, deciding against immediate Gemini Pro integration to maintain simplicity. The default remains `gemini-2.5-flash-image` due to speed, unless a "high-quality" request explicitly occurs. I've analyzed the request for "high-quality, professional", and decided `gemini-2.5-flash-image` should remain the default to maintain the "one-click" workflow. I'm now evaluating accessibility and responsiveness, with the current app design already performing well in this regard. I will update `ThumbnailForm.tsx` to include "Tone" (e.g., "Motivation", "Shock", "Focus") to help refine the output. My plan is: 1) Update `services/geminiService.ts` for detailed prompt instructions. 2) Update `components/ThumbnailForm.tsx` to add the Tone selector.

entries.description: Refined the Gemini prompt with strict anatomical, postural, and compositional requirements to ensure high-quality, realistic thumbnails with correct hand anatomy and title-matching expressions.
Updated UI for better title entry and a clearer headshot upload section to match professional quality standards.
Enhanced the results screen to better showcase the vertical 9:16 format with high-fidelity previews and a clean creator-focused interface.
